results.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>Ribonucleic acid (RNA) is an important biomolecule with diverse functions i.e. genetic information transfer, regulation of gene expression and cellular functions. In recent years, the rapid development of sequencing technology has significantly enhanced our understanding of RNA biology and advanced RNA-based therapies, resulting in a huge volume of RNA data. Data-driven methods, particularly unsupervised large language models, have been u

results.title: No Request Left Behind: Tackling Heterogeneity in Long-Context LLM Inference with Medha
results.tldr: Medha makes preemption practical with a co-designed set of mechanisms -- including Adaptive Chunking and Stream Pipeline Parallel that overcome the perceived inefficiencies and scaling challenges of chunking and a new parallelism strategy KV-Cache Parallelism to reduce the decode latency and afford interactivity despite very long context.
results.abstract: Medha makes preemption practical with a co-designed set of mechanisms -- including Adaptive Chunking and Stream Pipeline Parallel that overcome the perceived inefficiencies and scaling challenges of chunking and a new parallelism strategy KV-Cache Parallelism to reduce the decode latency and afford interactivity despite very long context.

results.title: Untie the Knots: An Efficient Data Augmentation Strategy for Long-Context Pre-Training in Language Models

results.abstract: Untie the Knots (\textbf{UtK}), a novel data augmentation strategy employed during the continue pre-training phase, designed to efficiently enable LLMs to gain long-context capabilities without the need to modify the existing data mixture is introduced.

results.title: Long Context, Less Focus: A Scaling Gap in LLMs Revealed through Privacy and Personalization

results.title: Spatio-temporal transformer traffic prediction network based on multi-level causal attention
results.abstract: <jats:p>Traffic prediction is a core technology in intelligent transportation systems with broad application prospects. However, traffic flow data exhibits complex characteristics across both temporal and spatial dimensions, posing challenges for accurate prediction. In this paper, we propose a spatiotemporal Transformer network based on multi-level causal attention (MLCAFormer). We design a multi-level temporal causal attention mechanism that captures complex long- and short-term dependencies f

results.title: UniCAIM: A Unified CAM/CIM Architecture with Static-Dynamic KV Cache Pruning for Efficient Long-Context LLM Inference
results.tldr: UniCAIM can reduce the area-energy-delay product (AEDP) by $8.2 \sim 831 \times over the state-of-the-art CIM-based LLM accelerators at the circuit level, along with high accuracy comparable with dense attention at the application level, showing its great potential for efficient long-context LLM inference.
results.abstract: UniCAIM can reduce the area-energy-delay product (AEDP) by $8.2 \sim 831 \times over the state-of-the-art CIM-based LLM accelerators at the circuit level, along with high accuracy comparable with dense attention at the application level, showing its great potential for efficient long-context LLM inference.

results.title: DELTA: Dynamic Layer-Aware Token Attention for Efficient Long-Context Reasoning
results.venue: Annual Meeting of the Association for Computational Linguistics
results.tldr: The results show that selective reuse of intermediate attention maps offers a robust path toward efficient long-context reasoning, and DELTA matches or surpasses full attention in accuracy, while reducing the number of attended tokens by up to $4.25\times and delivering end-to-end speedup.
results.abstract: The results show that selective reuse of intermediate attention maps offers a robust path toward efficient long-context reasoning, and DELTA matches or surpasses full attention in accuracy, while reducing the number of attended tokens by up to $4.25\times and delivering end-to-end speedup.

results.abstract: Experimental results show that the proposed architecture outperforms state-of-the-art methods for document-level aspect-based sentiment classification and the potential applicability of the approach for long document trust prediction is demonstrated.

results.title: Blind image super-resolution using swin transformer with unsupervised degradation and sparse attention
results.tldr: In this model, a degradation-aware residual Swin Transformer sparse attention block that is based on the Swin transformer layer, the non-local sparse attention (NLSA), and the degradation-aware convolutional (DA Cov) is proposed.
results.abstract: In this model, a degradation-aware residual Swin Transformer sparse attention block that is based on the Swin transformer layer, the non-local sparse attention (NLSA), and the degradation-aware convolutional (DA Cov) is proposed.

results.title: Sparse Attention across Multiple-context KV Cache
results.tldr: SamKV is presented, the first exploration of attention sparsification for multiple-context Key-Value Cache, which takes into account the complementary information of other contexts when sparsifying one context, and then locally recomputes the sparsified information.
results.abstract: SamKV is presented, the first exploration of attention sparsification for multiple-context Key-Value Cache, which takes into account the complementary information of other contexts when sparsifying one context, and then locally recomputes the sparsified information.

To address long-tail defect identification, we propose a coupled time–frequency attention model that accounts for the long-tail distribution and pervasi

results.title: DAM: Dynamic Attention Mask for Long-Context Large Language Model Inference Acceleration
results.venue: Annual Meeting of the Association for Computational Linguistics
results.tldr: This work introduces a dynamic sparse attention mechanism that assigns adaptive masks at the attention-map level, preserving heterogeneous patterns across layers and heads, enabling the practical deployment of large-scale Large Language Models without sacrificing retrieval performance.
results.abstract: This work introduces a dynamic sparse attention mechanism that assigns adaptive masks at the attention-map level, preserving heterogeneous patterns across layers and heads, enabling the practical deployment of large-scale Large Language Models without sacrificing retrieval performance.

results.title: Adamas: Hadamard Sparse Attention for Efficient Long-Context Inference
results.tldr: This work introduces Adamas, a lightweight yet highly accurate sparse attention mechanism designed for long-context inference that attains comparable or even lower perplexity than full attention, underscoring its effectiveness in maintaining accuracy under aggressive sparsity.
results.abstract: This work introduces Adamas, a lightweight yet highly accurate sparse attention mechanism designed for long-context inference that attains comparable or even lower perplexity than full attention, underscoring its effectiveness in maintaining accuracy under aggressive sparsity.

results.title: Challenging LLMs Beyond Information Retrieval: Reasoning Degradation with Long Context Windows
results.abstract: <jats:p>As Large Language Models (LLMs) increasingly accommodate larger inputs, context windows spanning hundreds of thousands or even millions of tokens are touted as promising for a wide array of applications. However, the potential decay in reasoning ability for larger inputs may compromise their utility. This study introduces a new benchmark called Find the Origin, which progressively tests the efficacy of LLMs on a simple intellectual task as the size of the context window increases. The te

Equipping Transformer with Random-Access Reading for Long-Context Understanding
results.tldr: This work proposes $\textbf{random access}$, a novel reading strategy that enables transformers to efficiently process long documents without examining every token, and Experimental results from pretraining, fine-tuning, and inference phases validate the efficacy of the method.
results.abstract: This work proposes $\textbf{random access}$, a novel reading strategy that enables transformers to efficiently process long documents without examining every token, and Experimental results from pretraining, fine-tuning, and inference phases validate the efficacy of the method.

results.title: Recurrent Memory-Augmented Transformers with Chunked Attention for Long-Context Language Modeling
results.tldr: A Transformer architecture for long-context language modeling that combines global attention with two biologically inspired components: chunked local attention and a gated FIFO memory mechanism, offering a lightweight and extensible design for tasks such as dialogue modeling, code completion, and document understanding.
results.abstract: A Transformer architecture for long-context language modeling that combines global attention with two biologically inspired components: chunked local attention and a gated FIFO memory mechanism, offering a lightweight and extensible design for tasks such as dialogue modeling, code completion, and document understanding.

Dispersed Hierarchical Attention Network for Machine Translation and Language Understanding on Long Documents with Linear Complexity
results.tldr: The proposed Dispersed Hierarchical Attention component captures the local context using a sliding window and the global context using a linearlyscaled dispersion approach and achieves O(N) linear complexity, where N is the length of the input sequence or document.
results.abstract: The proposed Dispersed Hierarchical Attention component captures the local context using a sliding window and the global context using a linearlyscaled dispersion approach and achieves O(N) linear complexity, where N is the length of the input sequence or document.

results.title: RouteWinFormer: A Route-Window Transformer for Middle-range Attention in Image Restoration
results.tldr: This work proposes RouteWinFormer, a novel window-based Transformer that models middle-range context for image restoration and introduces Multi-Scale Structure Regularization during training, enabling the sub-scale of the U-shaped network to focus on structural information, while the original-scale learns degradation patterns based on generalized image structure priors.
results.abstract: This work proposes RouteWinFormer, a novel window-based Transformer that models middle-range context for image restoration and introduces Multi-Scale Structure Regularization during training, enabling the sub-scale of the U-shaped network to focus on structural information, while the original-scale learns degradation patterns based on generalized image structure priors.

results.title: EMPIRIC: Exploring Missing Pieces in KV Cache Compression for Reducing Computation, Storage, and Latency in Long-Context LLM Inference
results.tldr: EMPIRIC is introduced as an oracle-based vision study, which explicitly defines theoretical bounds for accuracy, computation, and storage in KV cache compression and optimally balances computational efficiency, storage optimization, inference latency, and accuracy.
results.abstract: EMPIRIC is introduced as an oracle-based vision study, which explicitly defines theoretical bounds for accuracy, computation, and storage in KV cache compression and optimally balances computational efficiency, storage optimization, inference latency, and accuracy.

results.title: TAIL: A Toolkit for Automatic and Realistic Long-Context Large Language Model Evaluation
results.venue: Conference on Empirical Methods in Natural Language Processing
results.tldr: This work proposes TAIL, an automatic toolkit for creating realistic evaluation benchmarks and assessing the performance of long-context LLMs, and applies it to construct a benchmark encompassing multiple expert domains, such as finance, law, patent, and scientific literature.
results.abstract: This work proposes TAIL, an automatic toolkit for creating realistic evaluation benchmarks and assessing the performance of long-context LLMs, and applies it to construct a benchmark encompassing multiple expert domains, such as finance, law, patent, and scientific literature.

results.title: Extra Global Attention Designation Using Keyword Detection in Sparse Transformer Architectures
results.tldr: An extension to Longformer Encoder-Decoder, a popular sparse transformer architecture, is proposed and a method to selectively increase global attention is proposed and demonstrated for abstractive summarization tasks on several benchmark data sets.
results.abstract: An extension to Longformer Encoder-Decoder, a popular sparse transformer architecture, is proposed and a method to selectively increase global attention is proposed and demonstrated for abstractive summarization tasks on several benchmark data sets.

results.title: Context-Window Lock-In and Silent Degradation: How Conversation Loyalty, Attention Decay, and Sycophancy Compound to Erode AI Safety in Long-Horizon Interactions
results.abstract: <jats:p>Large language model (LLM) chatbots encourage users to maintain long, persistent conversation threads to preserve context. This paper identifies a compound failure mode we term context-window lock-in: users develop "conversation loyalty"-a preference for continuing within a single thread-while the model's retrieval accuracy, reasoning fidelity, and resistance to sycophancy silently degrade as the context window fills. The result is a false sense of continuity. The user believes the model

results.title: ACCT: Enabling Long-Context Transformer Reasoning Under Memory Constraints
results.abstract: In the rapidly evolving field of artificial intelligence, enabling transformer models to process long-context sequences efficiently remains a significant challenge due to high memory consumption and computational complexity. Traditional transformer architectures such as GPT and BERT rely on full self- attention mechanisms with quadratic complexity, making them inefficient for long input sequences and unsuitable for memory-constrained environments.ACCT (Adaptive Compressed Context Transformer)

Exploiting Quantum Entanglement for Sub-Quadratic Attention in Long Legal Documents
results.abstract: We propose QEAM, a hybrid quantum-classical attention architecture that breaks the well-known quadratic barrier in self-attention by routing information through structured entanglement circuits. Unlike prior quantum NLP work that largely reimplements classical dot-product attention on quantum hardware, our approach treats entanglement itself as the primary computational resource. The central idea is a four-level entanglement topology—spanning tokens, se

results.title: Visual Context Dual-Level Collaborative Transformer for Image Captioning
UTFormer: A UAV Sensing-based Triple Attention Transformer Network for Semantic Segmentation
results.abstract: Compared to the common urban landscape semantic segmentation, with the impact of flight altitude, UAV image semantic segmentation is more challenging because small targets have a very low percentage of pixels and multi-scale features. Yet, the commonly used successive grid downsampling strategy in the current transformer-based methods omits features of small targets. Furthermore, it leads to even worse results due to the complex background interference. In reac

results.title: Attention to the Environment and Long-Term Context Effects
Long-term Prediction of Sea Surface Temperature by Temporal Embedding Transformer with Attention Distilling and Partial Stacked Connection_supp1-3357191.docx
HARMONY: Hybrid Attention-Selective State Space Mixture for Long-Context Deep Learning
results.abstract: <jats:p>Modeling long sequences efficiently and accurately remains a fundamental challenge in deep learning. While attention-based architectures excel at capturing local and irregular dependencies, their quadratic complexity limits scalability to long contexts and large streaming workloads. Conversely, linear-time sequence models based on selective state space dynamics scale efficiently and exhibit excellent long-range memory, yet often lack the representational precision and adaptive compositio

results.title: RIS-Kernel: A Model-agnostic Architecture for Long-context LLM Inference via Sparse Attention
results.abstract: <jats:p>Full self-attention in large language models scales as O(N 2), limiting long-context document analysis to 65,536 tokens and requiring costly GPU clusters. The Reduced Interaction Sampling (RIS) inference engine addresses this constraint as a model-agnostic architecture. Without modifying weights, RIS reduces self-attention complexity to O(N log N) using sparse stochastic geometry that fits within commodity memory limits. We validate RIS on Qwen2-1.5B-Instruct across two regimes. In contr

Context triggers the retrieval of long-term memories into working memory to guide attention
results.abstract:
<jats:title>Summary</jats:title>
                <jats:p>How do we know what we are looking for in familiar scenes and surroundings? Here we tested a novel hypothesis derived from theories of human memory that working memory (WM) buffers mnemonic contents retrieved from long-term memory (LTM) to control attention. To test this hypothesis, we measured the electrical fields recorded noninvasively from human subjects’ as they searched for specific sets of objects in learned contexts. We found that

results.title: Recurrent Attention Transformer-based Optical Neural Network For Long-text Processing
results.abstract: <jats:p>We present a hybrid computational framework that integrates optical reservoir computing with transformer architectures, trained via a hybrid backpropagation-feedback alignment scheme, for sequence modeling and text generation. By leveraging the transient dynamics of the optical reservoir, temporal signals are preprocessed in the analog domain, enhancing feature saliency and reducing input complexity. Feedback alignment, a biologically inspired alternative to conventional backpropagation,

A Long Term Transformer-based Spatiotemporal Graph Attention Network for Traffic Flow Forecasting
results.abstract: <jats:p>Traffic flow prediction is the key to accurate urban traffic control and the basis for developing intelligent transportation systems. Recent studies have made substantial progress in traffic prediction by modelling complex spatiotemporal graph topology and considering sensors as road network nodes. However, the current spatiotemporal graph neural network model is limited by its structure. It can only utilize short-range traffic flow data and cannot effectively extract the long-term trend

results.title: HyPhyFormer: A Latent-Space Transformer with Hybrid Physics-Aware Attention and Adaptive Temporal Propagation for Long-Horizon Fluid Dynamics
results.abstract: <jats:p>Accurate long-horizon prediction of nonlinear dynamical systems governed by partial differential equations remains challenging for neural operator models, particularly under recursive rollout. The main challenges stem from limited multi-scale spatial representation and accumulated temporal errors, degrading stability and generalization. To address these challenges, we propose the Hybrid Physics-Aware Transformer (HyPhyFormer). This unified latent-space framework adopts a three-way decoup

results.title: BTHA: Block-Then-Hash Attention for Efficient Long Context
results.abstract: <jats:p>Long-context large language models incur substantial computational overhead during autoregressive decoding. Existing sparse attention methods can improve inference efficiency, but they typically rely on fixed sparse patterns, historical attention statistics, or coarse-grained proxy representations to estimate important KV positions, making it difficult to accurately capture query-dependent fine-grained relevance for dynamic KV retrieval. In this paper, we propose Block-then-Hash Attentio

results.abstract: <jats:p>Bridge deflection is a key indicator for structural health monitoring, and its accurate prediction relies on effectively capturing spatiotemporally coupled responses across long- and short-term time scales. Existing models struggle to simultaneously represent temperature-dominated long-period trends and load-induced short-term fluctuations, and often fail to fully exploit spatial correlations among multiple measurement points.In this study, the Menggu Nujiang Grand Bridge, a long-span st

results.title: Medium- and Long-term Forecast of Wind Farm Generation Power Based on Time Attention Transformer Approach
results.abstract: <jats:p>Accurate long-term wind power forecasting is crucial for ensuring stable power system operation and promoting renewable energy integration. However, existing forecasting models are often limited in their performance when processing wind power series due to inherent no stationarity and noise. They also face challenges such as inefficient modeling of long-sequence context and insufficient ability to capture long-term dependencies. To address these issues, this paper proposes a hybrid forec

ScaleFormer: Span Representation Cumulation for Long-Context Transformer
results.tldr: ScaleFormer (Span Representation Cumulation for Long-Context Transformer) is a simple and effective plug-and-play framework that adapts off-the-shelf pre-trained encoder-decoder models to process long sequences without requiring architectural modifications.
results.abstract: ScaleFormer (Span Representation Cumulation for Long-Context Transformer) is a simple and effective plug-and-play framework that adapts off-the-shelf pre-trained encoder-decoder models to process long sequences without requiring architectural modifications.

results.title: CGA-DCT: Cross-modal Gated Attention with Dialogue Context Transformer for Bi-modal Emotion Recognition in Conversation
results.venue: 2026 6th International Symposium on Computer Technology and Information Science (ISCTIS)

results.title: Long-Context Transformer Models for Meeting Summarization: A Comparative Study of Full Fine-Tuning and Parameter-Efficient Tuning
results.tldr: It is highlighted that PEFT effectiveness is highly model-dependent and that validation loss alone is an unreliable indicator of generative quality, which contributes a comprehensive benchmarking analysis and practical insights into optimizing long-document meeting summarization under computational constraints.
results.abstract: It is highlighted that PEFT effectiveness is highly model-dependent and that validation loss alone is an unreliable indicator of generative quality, which contributes a comprehensive benchmarking analysis and practical insights into optimizing long-document meeting summarization under computational constraints.

results.title: DIAS: Distance-based Attention Sparsity for Ultra-Long-Sequence Transformer with Tree-like Processing-in-Memory Architecture
Attention Itself Could Retrieve.RetrieveVGGT: Training-Free Long Context Streaming 3D Reconstruction via Query-Key Similarity Retrieval
results.tldr: This work designs a pose-aware spatial memory mechanism that organizes history frames according to their already estimated camera poses, enabling location-aware retrieval and proposes Segment Sampling so that the retrieval spans distinct relevant segments rather than a single high-similarity region.
results.abstract: This work designs a pose-aware spatial memory mechanism that organizes history frames according to their already estimated camera poses, enabling location-aware retrieval and proposes Segment Sampling so that the retrieval spans distinct relevant segments rather than a single high-similarity region.

results.title: Transformer XL Long Range Dependency Modeling and Dynamic Growth Prediction Algorithm for E-Commerce User Behavior Sequence
results.venue: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Algorithms for Computational Intelligence Systems (IACIS)

results.title: APCE: Adaptive Progressive Context Expansion for Long Context Processing
results.tldr: This paper proposes APCE as a context-aware solution to select the most important input chunks through low-dimensional semantic similarity matching with the current query to reduce the memory footprint and mitigate the ContextRot effects for long-context summarization tasks.
results.abstract: This paper proposes APCE as a context-aware solution to select the most important input chunks through low-dimensional semantic similarity matching with the current query to reduce the memory footprint and mitigate the ContextRot effects for long-context summarization tasks.

results.title: ResonatorLM: Causal Resonant Field Mixing for Efficient Long-Context Language Modeling
results.tldr: ResonatorLM is introduced, a new mechanism that replaces attention with a physics-derived alternative and replaces attention dot products with causal functions of damped resonators in a traditional network architecture and on standard long-context modeling tasks.
results.abstract: ResonatorLM is introduced, a new mechanism that replaces attention with a physics-derived alternative and replaces attention dot products with causal functions of damped resonators in a traditional network architecture and on standard long-context modeling tasks.

results.title: MomentKV: Closing the Directional Gap in KV Cache Eviction for Long-Context Inference
results.tldr: MomentKV is proposed, which maintains compact, small-size moment statistics over the evicted token set, including a count, key mean, value mean, and value-key covariance, and forms a mutually reinforcing loop between selective eviction and accurate correction.
results.abstract: MomentKV is proposed, which maintains compact, small-size moment statistics over the evicted token set, including a count, key mean, value mean, and value-key covariance, and forms a mutually reinforcing loop between selective eviction and accurate correction.

results.title: Context-aware heterogeneous graph neural networks with attention-based pooling for long-document summarization
results.tldr: A multi-level graph attention pooling (ML-GAP) strategy with input-conditioned scalar gates that hierarchically scores sentences by leveraging attention distributions from entity and topic nodes is introduced.
results.abstract: A multi-level graph attention pooling (ML-GAP) strategy with input-conditioned scalar gates that hierarchically scores sentences by leveraging attention distributions from entity and topic nodes is introduced.

results.title: MATCH: Modulating Attention via In-Context Retrieval for Long-Context Transformers
results.venue: Annual Meeting of the Association for Computational Linguistics
results.tldr: Empirical results show that MATCH significantly improves the performance of sparse-attention models on both synthetic and real-world natural-language tasks, and highlight the versatility of MATCH as a general approach for enhancing in-context retrieval capabilities while maintaining the efficiency benefits of sparse attention architectures.
results.abstract: Empirical results show that MATCH significantly improves the performance of sparse-attention models on both synthetic and real-world natural-language tasks, and highlight the versatility of MATCH as a general approach for enhancing in-context retrieval capabilities while maintaining the efficiency benefits of sparse attention architectures.

results.title: Octopus: Gated Selective Attention for Memory-Bounded Long-Context Inference in Large Language Models
results.tldr: O CTOPUS is proposed, a framework that confers fixed-memory inference onto pretrained Transform-ers without the information loss of linearization and outperforms state-of-the-art linearized baselines on the GSM8K benchmark, demonstrating that learned sparse retention serves as an effective regular-izer for long-horizon reasoning.
results.abstract: O CTOPUS is proposed, a framework that confers fixed-memory inference onto pretrained Transform-ers without the information loss of linearization and outperforms state-of-the-art linearized baselines on the GSM8K benchmark, demonstrating that learned sparse retention serves as an effective regular-izer for long-horizon reasoning.

results.title: A Comparative Study of Transformer-based and Hybrid Deep Learning Models for Long Document Summarization of academic research papers
results.venue: Al-Salam Journal for Engineering and Technology
results.tldr: A comparative study of two state-of-the-art summarization techniques shows that while Transformer models perform better in abstraction and fluency, the hybrid model has fact control ability that is much interpretable and aligns with document structure.
results.abstract: A comparative study of two state-of-the-art summarization techniques shows that while Transformer models perform better in abstraction and fluency, the hybrid model has fact control ability that is much interpretable and aligns with document structure.

results.title: CSTNet: a hybrid transformer and channel attention embedded UNet for single image dehazing
results.venue: International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Pattern Recognition
results.tldr: A novel Contextual and SaE-enhanced Transformer Network (CSTNet), which integrates global contextual modeling and semantic-aware enhancement into a UNetbased architecture and achieves better performance than benchmark methods in terms of PSNR and SSIM, while maintaining computational efficiency and high visual fidelity.
results.abstract: A novel Contextual and SaE-enhanced Transformer Network (CSTNet), which integrates global contextual modeling and semantic-aware enhancement into a UNetbased architecture and achieves better performance than benchmark methods in terms of PSNR and SSIM, while maintaining computational efficiency and high visual fidelity.

results.title: DepthWeave-KV: Token-Adaptive Cross-Layer Residual Factorization for Long-Context KV Cache Compression
results.tldr: Across LongBench, Needle-in-a-Haystack, L-Eval, and long-form QA and summarization benchmarks, DepthWeave-KV achieves near-full-cache task quality with substantially lower memory use, improving average score and retrieval accuracy over prior compressed caches.
results.abstract: Across LongBench, Needle-in-a-Haystack, L-Eval, and long-form QA and summarization benchmarks, DepthWeave-KV achieves near-full-cache task quality with substantially lower memory use, improving average score and retrieval accuracy over prior compressed caches.

results.title: The First Drop of Ink: Nonlinear Impact of Misleading Information in Long-Context Reasoning
results.tldr: This work systematically vary the hard-distractor proportion in fixed-length contexts, revealing a striking nonlinear pattern: as the proportion of hard distractors increases, performance drops sharply within the first small fraction, while the remainder of the range yields only marginal additional decline.
results.abstract: This work systematically vary the hard-distractor proportion in fixed-length contexts, revealing a striking nonlinear pattern: as the proportion of hard distractors increases, performance drops sharply within the first small fraction, while the remainder of the range yields only marginal additional decline.

results.title: Harmonic: Hierarchical State Space Models for Efficient Long-Context Language Modeling
results.tldr: Harmonic, a hierarchical state space model (SSM) for language modeling that maintains stable loss across sequence lengths 1K--8K on two independent clean benchmarks, while TinyLlama degrades catastrophically past its 2K-token RoPE limit.
results.abstract: Harmonic, a hierarchical state space model (SSM) for language modeling that maintains stable loss across sequence lengths 1K--8K on two independent clean benchmarks, while TinyLlama degrades catastrophically past its 2K-token RoPE limit.
